title: Ricinocarpus dimorphus
taxonomy: Ricinocarpus dimorphus is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rhamnaceae, which includes buckthorns and other related species. This plant is native to the tropical regions of Australia. Within the Rhamnaceae family, Ricinocarpus dimorphus is placed in the subfamily Ziziphoideae, which comprises approximately 700 species. The classification of this plant is based on its morphological characteristics and genetic relationships with other Rhamnaceae species. Further research and classification may be necessary to confirm the taxonomic position of Ricinocarpus dimorphus.

intro: Ricinocarpus dimorphus, also known as the 'Dimorphic Ricinocarp', is a plant species native to Australia, characterized by its unique dimorphic leaves that change shape in response to environmental conditions. The health benefits of Ricinocarpus dimorphus include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Ricinocarpus dimorphus is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and capsules, often used to support cardiovascular health and immune function.
